overview.title: Phishing-Based Cyberattack on Water Infrastructure
overview.summary: A basic-level cyberattack targeting a water and wastewater system through phishing, leading to remote access and disruption of industrial control systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Military espionage and sabotage against critical infrastructure

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attack begins with a phishing campaign targeting employees of the water and wastewater system. Employees receive emails containing malicious links or attachments designed to steal credentials or install malware. This initial access is facilitated through a vulnerability in web applications, allowing the attacker to compromise the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 555 Name: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: After gaining initial access through phishing, the attacker deploys remote access tools to establish persistent control over the compromised system. These tools allow the attacker to execute commands remotely, leveraging stolen credentials to access restricted areas of the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker manipulates the SCADA systems controlling the water treatment processes. By disrupting these systems, the attacker can interfere with the normal operations of the water and wastewater infrastructure, potentially leading to environmental harm or service interruptions.

Scenario.summary: The attack starts with a phishing email that tricks employees into granting access to the system. Once inside, remote access tools are deployed to maintain control and disrupt the industrial control systems, causing operational disruptions in the water and wastewater sector.
